summary: Fiery Estimated Prophet and strong second‑set jamming
blurb: The show shines with a blistering Estimated Prophet solo and a high‑energy second set that rolls through Eyes of the World, Stella Blue and Throwing Stones. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Phil locks down the groove, making this a solid late‑80s performance.
review: October 15, 1989 at Meadowlands Arena captures the Dead in a late‑’80s stride, delivering a set that’s uneven at the start but erupts into one of the tour’s most compelling second halves. The opening “Let The Good Times Roll” leads into a tight groove, while “Victim Or The Crime” shows Jerry occasionally searching for his place. The real fireworks arrive in Set 2: a scorching Estimated Prophet solo that fuels an exhilarating transition into a fast‑paced Eyes of the World, followed by hard‑driving drums and soaring improvisations on Stella Blue and Throwing Stones before closing with “Not Fade Away” and a heartfelt “Box of Rain.” Brent’s keyboards shoulder the Estimated Prophet section despite occasional tuning quirks, and Phil’s bass provides a rock‑solid foundation throughout. The crystal‑clear soundboard capture (Walker‑Scotton) presents the performance in vivid detail, making this show a must‑listen for fans who love high‑energy jams and classic Dead ballads.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising the Estimated Prophet solo, high‑energy second set and strong transitions; two recordings are mixed but still note solid playing despite subdued energy or keyboard tuning issues. No consistent negative comments appear, so a moderate positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard source that captures the full show with minimal static and no major dropouts, delivering the best representation of the performance.

key_highlights: Incendiary Estimated Prophet solo that drives the second set
Fast‑paced Eyes of the World transition into a tight jam
Strong ballad work on Let The Good Times Roll, Stella Blue and Crazy Fingers
Solid bass foundation from Phil and arena‑shaking drumming by Mickey and Bill

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a fiery solo on Estimated Prophet, adds funky MIDI effects and ‘dancing’ guitar on Stella Blue; occasional moments where he doesn’t fully lock in during Victim Or The Crime.
band_performance.Phil: Provides great bass work, especially noted on Box of Rain, laying down a solid foundation throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm guitar supporting transitions and jams, keeping the band grounded despite occasional keyboard tuning issues elsewhere.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work shines on Little Light and shoulders the Estimated Prophet section; some out‑of‑tune moments noted but overall strong playing.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums deliver arena‑shaking beats, driving the fast‑paced Eyes Of The World after a brief false start early in the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey to provide hard‑hitting, energetic drumming that propels the second‑set momentum.
